label: negative
text: [h1]Trigger warning for Fanboys![/h1] [h3]Part 1[/h3] If you're someone capable of forming their own opinion without blindly following drama-chasing streamers or YouTubers, or if you're not so deep into the gacha scene that you've lost all critical thinking, then you're at the right place. Let me be clear from the start: I wont sugarcoat anything here. Ill call things exactly as they are. As someone who played, whaled, and genuinely enjoyed this game from Day 1 but quit shortly before the 1.3 update due to a lack of real gameplay value, I returned in version 2.4 I can say this much: [i]Yes, the game is fun.[/i] [b]So why a negative review?[/b] Because once you take a step back and start questioning the systems, the mechanics, and the way this game is built. You realize pretty quickly how manipulative the design is at its core. In very short: FOMO - Limited-time banners and events designed to pressure Players Sunk Cost Fallacy - Ive already invested so much, might as well keep pulling Skinner Box Mechanics - Randomized rewards to condition behavior Whale Farming - Actively targeting players with addictive spending tendencies Endgame Bias - Late-game content favors those who constantly pull the latest banners. This isnt just "part of the genre" this is behavioral exploitation, aimed particularly at younger, vulnerable, or financially inexperienced players. And yet, every time someone brings this up, theres always that one guy who throws out the same old line: [spoiler]Well, its a gacha game.[/spoiler] Like what am i supposed to tell you? Thats like saying: [i]Well, hes a scammer. Its just what scammers do.[/i] Its not a defense. Its a deflection. Its a meaningless argument, mainly used in the wrong context. Heres why: Gacha isn't inherently predatory, thats a misconception. Gacha just means capsule toy machine. Gacha refers to random character or item pulls [u]not[/u] manipulative monetization and dont mistake RNG with exploitation. Time-limited banners, low drop rates, pay-to-roll systems are the exploitative choices not the genre requirements. Gacha isnt even a real genre if you think about it, its a monetization mechanic, rebranded as a genre to make exploitation sound normal. So no, the problem isnt gacha itself. Its just how developers choose to abuse it. And heres the ironic part: Players defend this game for being [b]less predatory[/b] than others. As if thats praise. As if less bad is somehow good. This only works because they [u]only[/u] compare it to other gacha titles never to fair games, never to ethical models. Its like ranking scams and calling the least harmful one generous. But when youve spent years in this ecosystem, being exploited, it starts to feel like a feature. You will stop asking why is this here? and start saying well, at least its not worse. [b]Now to be fair, the game does have its strengths[/b] Great combat system - but not if you're looking for deep systems Beautiful world design - no re-exploration value Characters that feel good to play - no you wont get them all A kind community - aslong theres no critic Tolerating mod support - if its not game breaking [b]But it also suffers from[/b] Horrible multiplayer Performance issues on the latest region Hard daily and weekly limits that kill meaningful long sessions An endgame that is boring and repetitive The predatory monetization model [u]Bottom line[/u] This game is not designed for everyone and definitely not for players who value respect or time. [b]So who is this game made for?[/b] Simple: for people whove already accepted having no gamer rights, soo mainly other gacha-only players i guess? [b]And who is this game [u]not[/u] made for?[/b] Players who: expect to have a choice expect fair design want to grind at their own pace want to finish endgame in less than 4 months want to play with friends in any meaningful way dont want to be drip-fed progress dont want to feel pressured dont want to feel bad for not playing the game [hr][/hr] [h1]Part 2[/h1] I said that i'm going to remove the "How to Improve part" in earlier statements if nothing happens, I waited long enough for improvements. Sooo, lets strip away the layers of polish and hype and dig into the ugly truth about Wuthering Waves in as much detail as possible, what it really is vs. what the fanbase believes it to be. [h2]Game vs. Framework[/h2] On the surface, WuWa looks like an open-world action RPG: flashy combat, cinematic story moments, big regions to explore. But underneath, it isnt structured like a traditional RPG. Its structured like a service-driven retention platform. The RPG elements are hooks, not goals. Exploration, story, bosses, echoes, progression, all of these exist less to offer a cohesive player-driven experience, and more to create loops that cycle players back into gacha banners and daily logins. Its not a game with gacha. Its a gacha framework wearing the skin of an RPG. [h2]Content vs. Retention[/h2] What players often call lack of content isnt an accident. Its by design. Waveplates (stamina), timegated progression, and rotating events are there to artificially stretch thin content. Instead of delivering full systems (meaningful multiplayer, deep progression trees), WuWa deliberately fragments content into drip-fed pieces that can be tied to monetization cycles. Every patch resets the loop: new character, new zone, new grind, while ignoring underdeveloped core systems. This creates the illusion of progress while avoiding real systemic depth. [h2]Monetization Dictates Design[/h2] Fans love to say WuWa is more generous than other gachas. That may be partially true in terms of early pulls, but generosity is only ever a calculated marketing strategy. Pity, banner rotations, and dupes are engineered psychological traps (FOMO, sunk-cost fallacy, Skinner box reinforcement). Stamina and grind exist not for game balance, but for monetization balance. Limited banners arent just excitement, theyre artificial scarcity to push spending. Even free 5-stars or bonus pulls exist as loss leaders to get you invested and onboarded before the real spending walls hit. If these systems dont stand on their own outside gacha, theyre not gameplay, theyre monetization infrastructure. [h2]Community Denial[/h2] The fanbase often masks these truths with defense lines like: Its generous. Its a gacha, what did you expect? Dont play it if you dont like it. They are listening a claim mostly based on surveys that only create the illusion of consent, making players feel their opinions matter while the devs use them to justify pre-decided plans. This deflection misses the point. The real critique isnt about whether WuWa is a gacha. Its about how every system is designed primarily for corporate goals, not player goals. The belief that WuWa is different or revolutionary is comforting, but functionally, its still running the same exploitative playbook. Just with shinier presentation and slightly smarter PR. [h2]The RPG Illusion[/h2] If you strip away the gacha, stamina, and monetization scaffolding, whats left? A 20-hour RPG with decent combat, okay bosses, mid puzzles, and a story with highs and lows. Nothing that inherently justifies timegates, monetization walls, or retention throttles. Thats the crux: the core game doesnt demand these systems. Theyre bolted on because thats what monetization requires. [h2]The Ugly Truth[/h2] Wuthering Waves isnt designed first to be played, its designed first to be stayed in. Everything else; combat, story, exploration exists to keep you circling the drain long enough to monetize you. My list of improvements i basically had here for long enough; https://steamcommunity.com/app/3513350/discussions/0/595158831570479481/ It's the biggest fake show I've ever seen, if anyone would ask me.

text: I played for about 59 hours, so this isnt a knee-jerk negative review. The game has real strengths: the combat feels good, the world is interesting, and at first it genuinely feels promising. Unfortunately, the longer you play, the more the progression systems completely undermine that experience. The gacha is extremely predatory. Materials are painfully scarce, and your world level increases faster than your character power, so instead of feeling rewarded for playing, you feel punished for leveling up. Progress doesnt feel earnedit feels restricted. The breaking point for me was realizing that no matter how much I playeddays, weeksI simply couldnt meaningfully pull characters. I liked a new character they introduced, kept playing consistently, and yet made zero real progress toward obtaining her. There was no sense of Im getting closer. Just effort with no payoff. Whats worse is that I didnt even consciously quit. I just stopped opening the game. The motivation disappeared naturally once it became clear that my time wasnt being respected. If you plan to spend money, you might have a different experience. If youre hoping for fair progression through play alone, be aware that this game may quietly burn you out rather than hook you. Im leaving this review so others can decide with open eyes.

text: I really wanted to like Wuthering Waves. And honestly the annoying part is the game itself is actually really good. Exploration feels great, like genuinely fun to just run around and play. The world looks Amazing, characters look good, music and voice acting are all solid. Combat is probably one of the best parts, its smooth and fast and way better than most gacha games Ive tried. Ive lost my 50/50 five times in a row now. Like actually five times. And every single time Im going all the way to 7080 pity. At this point pulling doesnt even feel exciting anymore, I already know Im going to hit pity and probably lose. You grind for Astrites, you save for weeks or months, finally pull hit 60, 70, sometimes to 75 , and then you lose. Again. Then you do it all over again. It just feels bad. Yeah sure, some people get lucky. Thats always how it is. But if your luck is even a bit bad, this system is just frustrating. Theres nothing fun about expecting disappointment every time you pull. And the worst part is what you get when you lose. The standard characters just feel outdated. Most of them are completely overshadowed and not even worth building compared to limited characters. They havent really changed or improved, so every time you lose a 50/50 its like ok cool, another character Im not going to use. Out of all of them maybe one is actually useful. The rest just sit there. So after losing five times in a row at high pity, yeah Im just tired of it. Theres no excitement left, no reward, just grind pity lose repeat and that sucks because the game itself is actually good. Like really good in some parts. text: This game is incredibly disappointing. Don't be fooled by the free tag; for those unaware this is a gacha game. I wouldn't even call it free to play, more so free to download. Before I get into the bad about this game however, let's start with the positives: 1. The exploration is incredibly fun, you'll have a great time running around killing enemies and solving puzzles all while being rewarded handsomely for doing so. 2. Alot of the character designs are incredible with amazing animations, satisfying gameplay and beautiful features. 3. The weapon gacha is 100% guaranteed, so no losing to some utterly useless weapon when pulling for a signature. 4. The environments are breath-taking across most of the game, accompanied with some amazing music, it really goes to show what can be achieved when gacha games really put effort into their game. 5. A separated character and weapon standard banner is a nice QOL feature that allows players to pull for what they need the most. The standard weapon banner is even permanently selectable, so you can pull copy after copy of some of the most free to play friendly weapons such as static mist or emerald of genesis, or even the new ones such as phasic homogenizer. 6. With your refunded pulls from 4* characters, you can save up to buy up to two copies of a 5* character, making R6 (C6) ing a character much easier and much more achievable for dolphins and just all players in general. Those are the major upsides of the game compared to other games in the genre. But they, in my opinion, are still not good enough reasons to download this game, start a fresh account, return to the game or what have you; especially with the downsides below. Onto the bad: 1. The optimisation is abysmal, every patch or two I notice a decent lag spike, my PC gets louder and I'll have to knock the settings down a level, which seems to be the general sentiment within the community. 2. The story is mid as all hell, most patches nowadays seem to follow the "gathering wives" formula of speedily building a relationship with the new character (who, more often than not, has never been seen before), the character essentially falling in love with the MC and then the MC drops them the millisecond their patch ends. 3. Because of the above, character quests are a thing of the past now; though I see this as an issue with gachas as a whole. There isn't much focus on the world as the newest wife for rover steals the spotlight and the main quest just turns into the character quest for them. Again, I think this is a gacha issue as a whole, such as ZZZ and HSR (the other two gachas I play) have also seemed to moulded the character quest with the story. 4. Once again relating to point 2, old characters are a thing of the past. Fan of Changli? Her last rerun was almost a year ago. Want shorekeeper? Same thing, except she was in a spending event where you had to pay I believe 150 to select her out of herself, Jinhsi and Casmellya. We rarely see old characters anymore, except from the one avengers assemble moment during the climax of Rinascita. Finally, the ugly. I'm aware alot of these issues pertain to gacha games as a whole, but they are why I wouldn't recommend gachas to anyone in general, lest this game (supposedly "generous"). 1. Powercreep finally seems to be kicking in. Granted it's not actually that bad compared to other gacha games, but be weary returnees as your Casmellya, Jihnsi, Jiyan and especially Yinlin will be nowhere near as effective as they once were. Wuwa does seem to be best for powercreep in my eyes, as I could still clear floors 3-4 with Carlotta Sanhua and Shorekeeper a few patches back, but now with the advent of tune break, the shill obviously caters towards the soul DPS of the archetype and you'll be struggling to clear TOA and WIWA due to such. Again, mainly a gacha issue as a whole, but wuwa is finally starting to show the cracks in such regard. 2. If you're unlucky, it sucks to suck. There is barely any fall back for being unlucky. Sure, you have the refunded corals from 4*s and 5050 losses, but it's not enough when characters now want their premium teams more nowadays. Many of the new characters just won't be enough without atleast one of their premium supports and their personal signature weapon. Take Cartethiya, she wants her weapon, Ciaccona and preferably Chisa. 4 cost team. Sigrika wants her weapon and Quiyuan, Zani wants Phoebe and Shorekeeper, ect. It's painful to lose constantly and there's no capturing radiance, nor are there enough pulls per patch really to save you from bricking your account. Even as a dolphin, I'm missing quite a few signature weapons and a few supports because my luck is genuinely abysmal. Same goes for other gacha games as per usual, and it happens to me in those games too. 3. Gambling. That's what gahca means for those unaware, and is why I called the game free to download as opposed to free to play. The above is one of the reasons why gambling is obviously bad, but sunk cost fallacy seems to be common within the space and is why I'll keep playing despite this review. 4. The anniversary rewards are genuinely pathetic, it actually disgusts me how bad they are and I can hardly put into words how much it does so. The words utterly pathetic are the best fits, but I'll explain more. There is 6 characters on banner this year, a step up from last year, but as you're about to see, overall it's about 10 steps backwards. The devs are rerunning the infamous cube event, which I've heard many bad things about and hated the event myself, in my spaces, nobody liked the event and I saw the same sentiment echoed online and when the event was announced to be rerunning. There is no character skins, only crappy bike skins that nobody asked for; all of which are paid. Who sat there and said "Lads, lads, I've got a BRILLIANT idea... BIKE SKINS! That's what everybody has been asking for!". No! Shut up! The pulls are pitiful too, 20 free pulls. Decent if it was a normal patch, but horrendous for an anniversary patch. Hell, patch 2.6 even gave 20 free pulls, and that wasn't an anniversary patch at all. There are still 5050s on the character rerun banners, no free 5*s, no news of our beloved 1.0 characters and even a good chunk of the 2.0 characters, nothing. Like I said, at least there are less characters on the rerun banners, but still, sh*te. People expected nothing and were still let down, including myself. Just go play HSR if you REALLY have to play a gacha game, atleast their anniversary has some decent rewards. The devs even had the audacity to say that they "learned their lesson" from last year's anniversary controversy. Yeah. Good one pal, april fools was over 2 weeks ago. 5. The toxic community. Kuro shills. Again an issue with all gachas, but it feels especially bad in this community Imo. Moral of the story: Don't play wuthering waves or any gacha game for that matter, even if they are supposedly generous. They aren't. The house always wins.

text: Wuthering Waves is a great game, especially for a F2P gacha title. It comes close to Expedition 33 in aesthetic and music, or even other AAA games. However, it has three major weaknesses: the 1.x stories havent been polished for a while, theres a lack of end-game content, and the UIs are poor made (turn off the UIs in Septimont and Ragunna to see how good this game could be without those UIs). Even so, with its current foundation, it deserves better. Leaving a negative review after paying and playing and for a long time feels contradictory, but I hope the developers notice that most new players struggle to make it through the 1.x stories because how boring it is. Updated, Spoiler Alert, only read if you've complete the stories and do 100% exploration: + 3.0 (and possibly next versions) is a solid 7.5/10. The main downside is that the story is very predictable and feels mid, not as unique or memorable as Cartethyia, Lupa, Canterella, Phrolova, Iuno, Augusta, Galbrena in 2.x and you know who used to be our Wayfinder and who has been keeping our Shores?. It's not even as good as Encore, they really nailed the 2.x stories and map designs (remember Journeying Paradise?), which fit the characters so well that I doubt they could have done it better. However, 3.0s map design is also outstanding in its own good way, but the school design isn't appeal to me, everything outside the school is gorgeous. Riding a motorbike around the map for exploration feels INSANELY good, the motorbike isn't as clunky as i thought, it literally could fly in the map, and the NPC designs are much better than in 2.x.

text: Let's start the review by saying this game is absolutely top tier. From the combat, to exploration to the increasingly enriching storytelling that is the lore of the game, It's definitely gonna pull you in once you start your journey in-game. However, as it stands, it's just a flashier and fancier version of gayshit impact. It's like the old analogy of the christmas gift with all their fancy and shiny wrappers. It may look pleasing to the eye at a single glance but once you open the gift, you're struck with the realization that it's just empty inside. . Let's start with the most blatant problem that I don't see any people talk about. The looping aspect of the gameplay. You're given 240 points of waveplates each day (if you let it accumulates that long) and what can you do with it? play 2 rounds of tacet fields (open field dungeon for echo tuning materials) or 3 rounds of materials domain (for character and weapon levelling) and ur done for the day. Heck, my playtime everyday consists of only 15 minutes or less gameplay. Now, imagine if I were to let's say splurge one day and just yolo it to max out the copies of character which would cost me like what?.....around 3-4k?.......what can i do with said character after that? record my nightmarish difficulty fights with hologram bosses and release youtube videos about it?.... oh wait, I think there's already an oversaturation of those kinda videos on the media. Hmmm, ah maybe I can record my big damage numbers and flex it on a youtube video. Wait..... alot of people are already doing that as well?....see the pattern yet?..........whenever any streamer "discuss" about the game it's only gonna be about how to maximize you damage on this hero or this new patch story content was fire and they're going into a new glory age with wuwa or "this is how i beat the endgame content for the current patch" kinda videos. Hmmm, where have I seen this pattern before....oh right....gayshit impact did this before and now wuwa is just riding along its coat tails. So, what i'm trying to justify here is why should I pay that much money to support a game that won't even let me play the damn game? Spending thousands of dollars only to have a couple of minutes of playtime each day is the epitome of stupidity in my humble opinion. Might as well have made the game be a subscription model instead of F2P KURO, cuz I don't feel like a Free2Play player and instead more like a Forced2Pay player to play your game at this point. . Now let's talk about the recent wonderful scheme the studio came up with in order to milk the player base bone dry. They came up with "synergistic team members effect". So, in order to have the maximum potential of a particular character, you would need to pull for these other characters that you don't like the design of, you don't like how they feel to play with or the character just doesn't interest you in general. However, you must pull these said character for you to fully maximize the potential of the character that you particularly like and would like to pull instead. I don't know if this is just a phase or an experiment they're trying to see how they can make more money but it sure as hell is scummy to say the least. . Next let's talk about skins/costumes which again is weird why no one is mentioning it but in my eyes, the costume does not look that different from the original base skin. However, the price they're charging for them is just like how blizzard handles the price of their recolor/retexture skins. If, this is the how they're gonna start off their journey with their skins/costume. I'm afraid that the ship is gonna sunk before it set sail. . In conclusion, the game at it's current state is still maintaining the precarious balance of not pissing me off too much as opposed to the good story content it fleshes out in each patch. However, In all other aspect of the game, be it levelling characters, world exploration, echo levelling and tuning, it already grinded me down hard and I'm slowly starting to despise it instead of enjoying it. As such, let's hypothetically say that if a person that have the same feelings as me as well being frustrated at the game just like me were to decide it's not worth it to pull new characters anymore since we would have to face these frustrations again and again when we get a new character, what would the studio do then to counter act this problem? I bet they'll just turn a blind eye to it just how gayshit impact did it. Such is the sad and true nature of a predatory gacha game. So, I cannot in good conscience, recommend this gambling den disguised as a game to new faces out there. . On a side note, I see lots of talk on the community of how this game is more "game" than gacha. I beg to differ cuz is it really more game? 15 mins of gameplay each day is more "game" than gacha? Having to gamble to get a good roll of stats on an echo is more "game" than gacha? For the casuals like myself, needing all those premium characters (again, gambling to get said characters) to clear endgame is more "game" than gacha?.
notes: Says top tier then dives deep into heavy negatives

text: OK, let me rant incoherently cuz I ain't happy with this game rn: Ever since Septimont came out, the game's performance unbearably bad. The new region (3.0) especially is unoptimized as hell. With my 9070 XT, I used to get around 100-120FPS in most situations. The new region? Make that 45-60FPS. I should never have to see these numbers with current gen in 1440p! Mind you, settings change nothing, it all renders with 14-31ms frame times no matter what settings I use. And it's not CPU bound as my CPU utilization never exceeds 30%, no - my GPU is almost always fully utilized tho. My guess is - poorly optimized geometry. And it doesn't look good either. In fact, it looks WORSE than 2.0. I can see NPC's popping in and out of existence when I'm walking around on-foot. I can see distant animations being rendered in like 5FPS. The overall aesthetic is less compelling too. Overall, I'm really struggling to see why this game's been on the receiving end of so much praise in general, not only performance-wise - I've played this game from day 1 using the original launcher until recently and so far, it's been on a steady decline from 2.3 onward. The story has reached it's peak at 2.2 and has been getting more convoluted and unnecessarily long since; the visuals have arguably become worse since; leveling characters has become harder and harder, the game has generally turned grindier since. I can go on and on, but you get the point. Anyway, can't recommend. Forget about gacha games in general - there are so many better single-player games out there it's not even funny.

text: TLDR; I dont think this game is worth your time, its a mixing pot of ideas from other better franchises designed to maximise engagement rather than produce a best-in-class or unique experience, its fine to kill some time, but that time could almost certainly be better spent elsewhere. As a foreword for the rest of the review, I want to note that for this type of game there is a temptation to lower the bar for what you might consider good due to some combination of: its free*; there is so [i]much[/i] game; they will fix it later/just give it time to get good; and its better than these other (terrible) gatcha games. When I look at a game, I care about what makes it a different and worthwhile experience now. With that said: Pros: - It has a very large open world to explore that looks nice visually - It is effective at distracting the player with random busywork and so is useful for killing time - It is a babies playmat of extrinsic rewards with infinite checklists of things to do Cons: - It has the story of a budget isikai anime loosely stabled to a badly written student essay on what if souls were sound - The writers forgot that it helps if characters have personalities, back-stories and are part of the setting as opposed to being pin-up posters - The open world is littered with activities that look like they were placed by a random number generator, breaking the illusion of the world. - The combat while looking like it has a reasonable foundation rapidly becomes repetitive and subsequently gets buried under particle-effect maelstroms - It has non-sensical gatcha monetisation, i.e. a character will cost somewhere between 1 and 500 levels of absurd randomness. Everything the game tries to get you to buy, including the monthly subscription, are objectively terrible value for money I really wonder how peoples sense of value can become so skewed as to make this business model work. To wrap up, this is a rushed game with the devs on a content treadmill strapped to a grindy gatcha machine. Not all gatcha games are bad (although the gatcha business model is still a strong signal to stay clear), Arknights and Limbus Company come to mind. But those games find a unique identity separate from their business model. Wuthering Waves is core identity is a Genshin Impact clone with some good ideas but poor execution.

HORRIBLE material farming RNG that is MEANT to waste your PRECIOUS time while giving you nothing or barely half of what you actually want. You can purchase the subscription multiple times but the rewards dont stack and if you miss one day you will miss that days reward which is a predatory tactic that triggers your FOMO. The world looks beautiful but plays like a walking simulator making it feel empty and boring. It looks alive, but doesn't feel like it. Quick time events (QTES) are mindless and pointless: no stakes, no tension, just button-mashing to keep your character from freezing mid-air. (peak story-telling inspired by Sony games lol) Male characters are rare to the point of being an afterthought. Criticizing the game is basically a taboo within the fanbase (like most gacha/anime fanbases). Overall, the game does have potential, but I doubt the story will ever improve since it caters to a specific demographic. As long as thats the case, dont expect the story or events to get any better. Being free to play does not exempt it from criticism. (which it isnt if you actually think about it since you pay with your time and these gacha mechanics earn them millions of dollars every month that other triple-A games could only dream of) If youre fine with collecting waifus like Pokemon, grinding Astrites and materials through boring chores, slogging through mediocre stories (There's a reason why they added a skip button), doing repetitive and unchallenging events like a masochist, then this game is right up your alley.

label: negative
text: [h1]Wuthering Waves Honest Review (~1000+ hours played)[/h1] Playing since launch (May 2024). Linked Steam in July 2025. All endgame content attempted with heavily invested teams (S2 Cartethyia, S1 Zani and others in premium compositions). [hr][/hr] [h2]What's Good[/h2] [b]Presentation[/b] Character designs, combat animations, voice acting, and music are genuinely excellent. One of the best-looking action gacha games out there. [b]Main Story[/b] The main plot is engaging and the primary reason to keep logging in at all. [b]Combat Feel[/b] Fast, responsive, skill-expressive combat with Intro/Outro skill chains and aerial combos. Better than anything the competition offers in this regard. [b]Open World[/b] Exploration is fun, the map is well-designed, and the variety of elite enemies and bosses is satisfying early on. [hr][/hr] [h2]Echo System The Core Problem[/h2] The Echo system is the artifact grind of Wuthering Waves and it is deeply broken at the endgame level. [b]Triple RNG lottery[/b] A usable Echo requires the right Sonata set, the right main stat, [i]and[/i] decent substats. Most echoes are immediately trash by the first or second filter. [b]Tuner scarcity[/b] Tuners (the material required to unlock Echo substats) are critically scarce. With roughly 80 tuners per day maximum from Tacet Fields which isn't always possible to prioritize you cannot tune echoes fast enough to keep up with what you farm. The result: thousands of level-zero echoes clogging your backpack with no way to evaluate them. [b]3,000 echo backpack limit[/b] With no way to evaluate untuned echoes and not enough tuners to tune them, the backpack hits the cap constantly. You can't farm new ones until you delete old ones you haven't been able to assess yet. It's an impossible cycle. [b]No first-substat preview[/b] There is no way to see an echo's first substat without spending tuners on it. A simple preview before tuning would eliminate most of this pain. [b]Deprecated Tacet Fields[/b] Older Sonata sets are power-crept, their farming domains are largely pointless, and new character-specific sets are added every patch with no catch-up mechanism for veteran players. [b]Echo management UX[/b] No echo presets to swap full sets for characters in one click. Some improvements were made, but they are meager. No auto-lock filters that persist between sessions. Managing 3,000+ echoes manually is a second job. Gamble inside a gamble, with a scarce resource gating access to even evaluating your drops. The worst part of the game and it has been this way since launch. [hr][/hr] [h2]Endgame DPS Checks and Zero Flexibility[/h2] The endgame has shifted from a skill test into a pure DPS check with rigid, memorized rotations. [b]Tower of Adversity (ToA)[/b] and [b]Whimpering Wastes (WhiWa)[/b] are both timed challenges where losing doesn't mean your Resonators were defeated it means you didn't deal enough damage in time. The timer is the only mechanic that matters. [b]Forced mono-element teams[/b] Meta compositions like mono-Havoc or mono-Fusion require pulling up to three limited 5-star Resonators who only function together. Missing one makes the whole team non-viable and the others you already pulled less useful. [b]Character shilling[/b] ToA and WhiWa buffs are specifically tailored to the newest Resonators each patch, making older invested units underperform even at high Resonance Chain levels. [b]New mechanics with no legacy support[/b] Tune Rupture and Tune Strain introduced in later patches provide no benefit to older characters and have no clear in-game explanation. Veterans are effectively locked out of optimized endgame gameplay. [b]Rotation rigidity[/b] The optimal way to play is a memorized button sequence. Deviating from the Intro/Outro skill chain for any reason getting interrupted, switching characters out of order, making a single mistake cuts significant DPS. There is no room for improvisation. It plays like a song you must perform perfectly on repeat. The best endgame content this game ever had was [b]Virtual Crisis[/b] no timer, strong enemies, real creative flexibility to experiment with compositions and strategies. It was fun. That mode has never returned. [hr][/hr] [h2]Content and Events[/h2] [b]Side quests[/b] are long, mostly unvoiced, and not worth the time. Unvoiced dialogue in a story-driven game is a signal that the content doesn't respect the player's time. [b]Events[/b] have shifted away from combat challenges toward mini-game filler puzzles, platforming, short disposable experiences with no replayability. No meaningful battle events for months at a time. [b]Time-limited everything[/b] Timed challenges, timed events, daily energy tied to real-world timers. The game punishes not logging in every day and offers little for players who want to engage on their own schedule. [hr][/hr] [h2]Quality of Life[/h2] [b]Damage stats[/b] No calculated skill damage shown in the stats menu based on current Resonator attributes. Comparing echo builds requires manual calculation. [b]Food and buffs[/b] Unusable in ToA, which is exactly where you need them most. Useless everywhere else because open-world enemies don't require them. [b]Character test room[/b] Requires fully exiting and re-entering to reset, making it tedious to test different builds or abilities. You can't test characters that are not in gacha rotations. If you are new, you might have to wait a whole year to test some characters (and acquire them). [b]Alerts[/b] "Do not show again" alerts settings do not persist between sessions. Every login requires checking them in. [hr][/hr] [h2]Verdict[/h2] [b]Played:[/b] ~1,000+ hours since May 2024 launch [b]Friends still playing:[/b] 0 out of 5 [b]Recommended for[/b] casual players who enjoy the story, open-world exploration, and character collection without caring about endgame performance. [b]Not recommended for[/b] players who care about endgame progression, build optimization, or expect meaningful returns on long-term investment. The Echo system and increasing power creep will eventually drive you away as they did every friend I started playing with. The game had a strong identity: fast, expressive, skill-rewarding combat that genuinely set it apart. Patch by patch, it has traded that identity for mobile gacha conventions daily energy timers, mandatory meta teams, power creep, and an artifact grind that is in some ways worse than its main competitor. The main story still has pull, but it is holding together a game that increasingly doesn't respect the player's time.

text: This is about the 4th GACHA that I've played that follows this same hook and set pattern... and I just really don't like it, there's a core concept at play and for me it turns everything else sour. The game could look great (it does), play great (not bad), have a good story (its not), fun characters (they're not) with good voice acting (nope)... none of that matters to me once I've decided that a core concept is something I fundamentally don't like. Not anyone's fault I guess. They made it this way, and I don't like the way they made it... who's to blame really. They can blame me, I can blame them, you can blame me, I can blame you... all pointless. Moving on. Back to my point: In a "GACHA character collecting" kinda thing like WuWa standard characters are... okay, I guess. You could totally just play the entire game with them and... that would be alright, kinda... but wow those new characters look amazing, are OP and just genuinely more fun and...that's the hook set in your lip, too late now you're basically already caught. Fish Dinner. Your wallet. Now the pattern kicks in. Quickly trying to explain the pattern most if not all of these games use: Not only do you need to get lucky or spend a lot currency pulls to get the character, but then you need to get them again, multiple times or they are straight up subpar... then also on top of that you need to get their weapon... and then also get their weapon multiple times as well. Oh and there's a time limit to do all of this during, and probably an extra thing or two like farming specific events or bosses for more currency or items on a daily limit and a timer as well. Of course you could just open up your wallet to fix one or all of these constraints but... that's the whole point. If you wanna just have some casual fun with stellar graphics in a weird sci-fi gibberish story with mostly annoying characters that won't make sense or shut up and choices that don't matter at all even a little bit... you can do that for free and that's nice, I guess. It's a good price if you can resist the pressure to use real money. However, if you wanna actually create a powerful maxed out upgraded team of characters that you actually like... you're in real trouble. You need to either turn WuWa into a full time job or spend an obscene amount of real money. Even if I do it myself, there's no way I'd ever recommend any of this to anyone else. It all looks, sounds, and feels like a multilayered system of hooks and FOMO.

text: You might be wondering, why I would leave a negative review on a game that I have played for 90 hours. Wuthering Waves is a beautiful game, with a fun combat system and great world design, which makes it great to sink in time. But the main thing about it, which is also why I cannoty possibly ever give it a positive review, is that it is a gacha game. This means, instead of unlocking new characters or weapons naturally, you need to gamble for them on the gacha. The game does provide free currency, but it reminds you constantly that you could just spend money and try your luck to get what you want. And in addition to that, the characters are not guaranteed. When you eventually get a 5 start character, there is only a 50% chance it is the character you want. Every aspect of the entire game ties back to the gacha system. While WuWa is fun, if you have not played a gacha game yet, I would heavily advise you to just stay away and play games that aren't trying to get you to gamble on pixels and pull you pack in with FOMO.

text: [For context, I've played this game every day since its release up until 3.0 and as a result will be a long read. I had been incredibly emotionally invested in this game for almost two years and I have not been more disapointed in anything ever] [b]As of 3.0 there is no longer a single merit I can provide to this game, maybe apart from the fact it looks nice graphically.[/b] [h1]Story[/h1] The plot has always been bad, but now they've committed to an episodic per patch sort of storyline that feels the need to start and complete a story within the same version. This is why we rarely get reoccuring characters and if we do they are mere fanservice and don't do much for the plot (2.7 was meant to be the finale of Rinascita and yet it centred around a character that was introduced in that very version.) The story beats, and especially the character arcs and lore are all just recycled from things we're used to in other media. The kids all have dead parents, Chisa is a young girl with powers that the people where she grew up in rejected so she was lonely. Fluerdelys's war against the dark tide and the result of it is almost identical to that of Artorias of the Abyss from Dark Souls. Of course none of these ripped plots actually come with any of the major consequences of it. No playable character in WuWa has ever been close to death. Cartethyia in 2.7 and Phrolova in 2.5 had fake deaths so obvious that it failed to get any emotion out of me, and for those who actually believed it the game made sure to tell you they're not really dead five whole minutes later. In simple terms, WuWa's story is so afraid to tread any new ground, it would rather recycle old tried and tested stories and plots with none of the consequences. WuWa is so afraid to make its player uncomfortable or upset that it's boringly safe. I should also add, Rover might genuinely be the worst protagonist I have ever witnessed. They're so pathetically the losers fantasy. Super OP being that is universally loved and has history with literally everyone despite being an amnesiac and has the personality of cardboard. Atleast Genshin understood their protagonist was boring and didn't make them constantly the centre of attention. I do not care one bit for Rover. [h1]Gameplay[/h1] "Gacha game stories suck lol no one's here for that" The gameplay unfortunately isn't much better. The story plot has always been ass but the gameplay was once really engaging. In the 1.x versions, and very early 2.x (2.0 - 2.3) we used to very often get characters that were very versatile and could be used in a variety of teams, this was then furthered by the quickswap system, which is a fancy way of saying that characters would finish their animations even if swapped off. This meant a high level player was able to essentially play multiple characters at the same time, and because base damage was always better than buff stacking it ended up being the damage ceiling of the game. Skill expression? In my gacha game? Oh hell no. Camellya was the first, her long skills would not continue off field. This was a change implemented half way through the beta, I remember the single video we got of a Camellya quickswap team before this change was implemented. After Camellya the next character to be particularly egregious was Zani, Zani needed one of two specific characters to use 70% of her kit and of those two the paid option was blatantly far more powerful than the free one, and the free one was a heavily contested character that everyone wanted in some form. Just two examples, but fast forward to 3.0 and quickswap is dead. The fusion team is a foul imitation of it that doesn't even imitate it well because it is so railroaded, the game does all but explicitly tell you the rotation. "Eh, I don't really care about high level gameplay I don't play the game like that anyway" The events are trash too, and got worse alot earlier than the rest of the game. If you started the game in 2.0 or later you have not experienced even one of the top 3 combat events. But for times sake I'll focus on the Depths of Illusive Realms mode. This was a rougelike mode that had the player stacking buffs as you'd expect from a mode such as this but there was also kit enhancements which for example would give Xiangli Yao 6 hypercubes instead of 3. The 1.4 version of this event even included difficulty modifiers which allowed the player to finetune their own experience. If this wasn't enough, underlevelled characters would be automatically levelled up for this mode. I cannot overstate how good this mode is it was all the best aspects of WuWa in a single gamemode. Long term replayable content. Kuro removed it. They said they would rework it, and they did. It returned in 2.3 as Dreaming Deep. Which was honestly a perfect representation of WuWa as a whole from that point. It did have a good core, and it's mechanical changes were an improvement but it didn't matter. The mode was 95% text and loading screens, and the difficulty modifier is gone and the default difficult is so easy if you actually read and coordinate your buffs. In general the events all followed this trend, if you think of them as a steak. Early events had so much meat and a nice amount of fat, the later ones had Almost no meat and was just all fat. The 2.7 vampire survivors knockoff was one of the worst events I have ever had the misfortune of playing. The next few sections will be much shorter. [h1]Exploration[/h1] "I dont care about combat I just want to walk around exploring" The open world is so streamlined that the game doesn't even allow you to explore. If the devs want you to go somewhere you WILL go there. Of course the 1.x maps did actually have meaningful exploration that you would be able to discover by yourself but as the trend goes. Any meat this game had was eroded with updates. [h1]Character designs[/h1] WuWa has no identity and this issue is no clearer than in the character designs. It's all just copy paste designs from better media. Think Chisa and Ryoko from Kill-la-kill. Or Galbrena and Bayonetta. This isn't character design related but it does showcase the spirit. Scar's phase 2 directly copies moves from Morgott, the Omen King, and Maliketh, the Black Blade. The designs are like this, I'm not sure if Kuro have ever had an original Idea. Oh and all the designs suffer from the need to be sexy. Augusta should be wearing greaves but how would we showcase her legs. Fluerdelys should actually look like a holy warrior, Lupa should look like a gladiator at all, You get the point. [h1]Community[/h1] WuWa's community is so extraordinarily bad, its full of disgruntled Genshin fans that will literally eat up anything Kuro do because it's not Hoyo. If the person hasn't played Genshin then usually their whole personality is hating hoyo. On top of this the community is just like Rover with no personality. The only community joke is "Gathering Wives" because they're lonely tards. [h1]Conclusion[/h1] WuWa feels like the Solo Levelling of video games. It looks pretty and has the hype moments and aura, but the moment you start thinking about literally anything in this game at a deeper level than just the surface you release how vapid and void of any personality it is. Don't waste your time, play better games.

label: negative
text: Wuthering Waves - Style over Substance, Gacha Rot at Its Core Its a gacha game. That kinda says it all. The core problem with Wuthering Waves is the same one plaguing most gacha games: wasted potential buried under bloated systems, RNG hell, and monetization that actively fights the player experience. If this game ditched gacha entirely and went for a Warframe-style model instead, it couldve actually stood out. Instead? Youve got a convoluted mess of grind, garbage rates, and characters that mostly suck. About 90% of the roster is unusable filler. F2P characters are especially underwhelming, Rover (the main character) is the rare exception who doesnt feel like hot garbage. Crafting? Useless bait. Feels like its only there to trick you into burning resources. In Warframe, crafting actually matters, you can build just about everything with a bit of grind, and most of it is fun and viable. Here? Even 5-star gacha weapons feel mid because theyre hyper-specific and barely work outside certain builds. The craftable ones? Dont even bother. And that brings me to the bigger picture: Wuthering Waves and gacha games in general, could learn a LOT from how Warframe respects players' time and wallets: - You can just buy the characters and weapons you want. No RNG. No gambling. - 200+ weapons with unique alt-fires, skill sets, and deep modding systems. It feels like youre building a loadout, not just stacking numbers. - 100+ characters, all solo-viable. Team synergy helps, but isnt mandatory. - Massive cosmetic variety, skins, FX, attachments, color swaps. - Traversal is fun: flying, hoverboards, mobility that feels good. - Crafting is meaningful. Premium stuff can be earned just by playing. - Theres actual trading. Got a rare item you dont need? Sell it to another player. - Ultimate packs with maxed-out characters + cosmetics cost about $99 AUD. Compare that to Wuwas $200 gamble for a chance at the character you want. - Weapons are not character-locked. Want a flamethrower or gravity cannon? Use it on whoever. Plus: Warframe has a Vault system. Limited characters rotate out but come back yearly with special events that let you grind for them. No FOMO, no expiration BS. Meanwhile, Wuthering Waves has... a few overpriced cosmetics, some flashy animations, and a lot of style over substance vibes. Sure, its pretty but that only gets you so far when the systems underneath are built to waste your time unless you open your wallet. The devs have talent, clearly. The combats fun, the world is promising, and the aesthetic slaps. But if they keep pretending this gacha model is the only way forward, theyre just holding the game back from what it could be.

text: I am recommending this game. It is by all means a good game. Visualy it is satisfying. Gameplay is fluid and fast, unlike in other games of the same type. Music/sounds are also very nice. You might also like the story, it's not very deep, but it's fine. Map puzzles are nice, and from ten to twenty hours into the game, you'll be given a tool to find most of the hidden stuff you've missed, which is very great for the obsessive treasure hunters like myself. You can capture the monsters and use them as the equivalent of gear to boost the stats of your characters. Good stuff ! But then, why a negative review ? For me, there's a lack of the sense of progression. At the start, you'll have your main character that you'll be able to level up, upgrade his abilities, and have some good basic weapon until the level 45 of your character. You'll also unlock several affinities for your main, and he/she will remain relevant for a long time. It won't be enough, though. After 100+ hours of gameplay, I only have 3 decently upgraded characters. To upgrade some aspects of your characters, for instance their abilities, you have to fight specific bosses. Different bosses for different characters because of the different drop. But the hard limit on some of those bosses is only 3 times a week. Good luck with that if you need to upgrade several characters, which you will have to do. Next issue is still the lack of progression. You start as a weak level 1 character in an area full of weak mobs. Every 10 levels or so, you'll be able to unlock new content, you'll be able to push your characters 10 levels higher, as well as their weapons and "gear". Again, it's a bit of a grind, but managable. HOWEVER, MONSTERS LEVEL UP TOO ! The weakest of monsters were grinding the same god-level boss dragon that you did ! Why, why on Earth does it work like that ? So, basically, with each level the only change you see is the number of damage you deal to the mobs. And yet, the time it take to demolish them overpowerd weaklings remains the same. How is this progress ? It's a very annoying feature. Funny enough, there are game mods that do have difficulty levels that you can pick, and increase gradually, But that doesn't apply to the regular monsters for some reason. After being humiliated, the monsters in the starting location were probably pumping iron 24/7 and waiting for a rematch with your sorry azz. About GACHA system (sigh)... At first, while you'll be discovering secrets, solving puzzles, and progressing through the story, you'll feel like you have a rather generous amounts of currency to play gacha. You'll think that maybe you have enough to get and upgrade a character you want. You go to the gacha page and... don't see the character you want, because it's not their turn to be unlocked in the gacha event yet. The pair of characters changes every 2 weaks or so. And good luck getting something good, if you rushed your decisions and played the beginner banners ! But if you are lucky like me, the needed character banner might appear at just the right moment. 1600 of currency for 10 spins. To fully upgrade a character, you either need to get him 7 times, or 5 and then buy missing parts in a specific shop. One also needs to add the weapons for the character : + 7 spins on a different banner. Guaranted character is available after 80 spins. With nearly 40000 currency for gatcha, I was able to get 1 weapon and 3 times the character. I think I was lucky. The fun part is, after you get the character you want, there are high chances that you won't be able to use it right away. Not because of that limit of 3 boss battles per weak I've mentionned before, nope. You won't be able to use it, because the upgrade materials are locked by the story content. Awesome ! But I need the character to progress in some side content... *sigh* But wait, there's more ! You play with a set of 3 characters. Usually, those are used together to get the best out of the group, where one powers up another in a chain-like system. So, when the gacha banner comes (and they come in pairs - two banners for two characters), chances are you'll need both of those to get the best of the character you want ! Good luck grinding that currency ! You'll have 15 days until the banners change again. To be more clear, you don't need new OP characters to progress in the game content, but they will sure make your life easier, especially if compared to the starting characters. However, some content, such as Tower of Adversity - where one can get some gacha currency, does require having at least 9 leveled up and geared caracters to finish it. Good luck with that grind ! In the end, WUWA starts as a fun and interesting game. However, the lack of the sense of progression makes it boring rather swiftly.

text: The game, when you're allowed to play, is pretty damn good. The story, side quests, characters, sounds design, world design, combat... all of it is fantastic. There are two major flaws this game has that stops it from being a 10/10 and drags it down to a solid 5/10.... Gatcha and stamina mechanics. You can have a decent gatcha monetization model that isn't fomo based and incredibly predatory but I have yet to see one and this game is no exception. It's bad and it should feel bad. Stamina is another tactic used by devs to get you to log in regularly and force you to form habits so you're more likely to engage with their monetization model: Gatcha. The main problem is that because these systems exist it strangles it from doing anything else. If it had the freedom to have an actual end game content loop that you WANT to engage with instead of just logging in to put a nickle into your gatcha fund then it would be insanely better. In the end the game is free and it's worth picking up just to enjoy the main story, side stories and to fly around to enjoy the world and music. Just keep in mind what I said above and curve your expectations. "If you're saying it's worth picking up why don't you recommend it then?" Because I f*cking hate stamina systems. It really is such a dumb thing to put in a game.

label: negative
text: Ok, im gonna break it down for everybody. Game is locked to 60fps if you have ryzen 5 of any kind. And requires 8 cores 16 threads to unlock 120fps. Game keeps pushing visuals, and optimization keeps getting worse. To the point nowadays if I do not use lossless scaling 3x, then the game regularly triggers LFC on my VRR monitor. Which makes input lag quite bad. AFMF 2.1, and in game frame gen...are horrible. The game runs so poorly, im actually thinking of dropping it. Because of just how badly it runs and is optimized. Game smoothness, should ALWAYS come first over visuals. If you cant make a mobile game run good on PC. Then you dont need to be pushing out visual updates, that push the envelope. AMD 9000 series 600 dollar gpu. 10 months after launch, no ray tracing support. And horrible performance worse than my old 5700xt. And...we're talking 10 months after release. On a gpu i get 120fps+ on cyberpunk at max settings with rt enabled. There is no excuses that will do. They keep pushing things further, and making the game worse and worse performance wise. And they need to take 2 months if need be, and actually optimize this game somehow so people can play it smoothly. Smoothness is KING when it comes to video games. Nobody wants a stuttery slideshow and issues when playing your game. Needs fixed badly. Just take the time Kuro, it needs it so badly. I cant even stress it enough. People are dropping the game left and right because of the ridiculously bad optimization. And ignoring of the problem by Kuro, while also pushing the visuals further, making the problem even worse! Overall, i would AVOID this game, unless you really really hate yourself. For the endless optimization issues alone, not even mentioning 1 char+weapon can cost you upwards of 600 dollars if you luck is bad. Yeah no. Just save your sanity. And play something else. Its really just not worth your time. Unless you just like to waste loads of money, and have performance issues literally all the time. Getting LFC "only triggers below 48fps, WHILE im using 2x frame gen. Means drops go as low as 23/24. Pretty inexcusable. And im considering quitting until they actually put some work into what is needed to keep it running smoothly. Instead of just making a new character and asking me for my rent money.

label: negative
text: The story is an incoherent mess. It cannot hold onto a single idea for more than a few minutes before yanking your attention somewhere else with zero buildup, zero payoff, and zero respect for the players ability to follow along. One moment you are supposed to care deeply about a plot thread, the next the game abruptly pivots to something unrelated and expects you to just accept it. The result is narrative whiplash. You are constantly left asking why anything is happening at all. The UI and progression systems are even worse. This game is absolutely buried under menus. Menu inside menu inside menu. Dozens of currencies, tabs, notifications, red dots, daily chores, weekly chores, event chores. Just speccing a character feels like filing paperwork. Collecting daily rewards turns into a scavenger hunt through badly organized screens. It feels deliberately bloated to keep you clicking rather than playing. Combat is flashy but brain dead. Strip away the effects and animations and what you are left with is a glorified button masher. There is no real depth, no meaningful strategy, and no demand for mastery. You mash buttons, enemies fall over, repeat. It looks impressive at first glance but collapses immediately once you realize how shallow it actually is. And lets be honest about what this game really is. It is a waifu gooner simulator wearing the skin of an action RPG. Fan service is not a bonus here, it is the foundation. Character designs and animations are doing all the work because the gameplay and story cannot stand on their own. If you are not here for that specific appeal, there is very little left to justify your time. In the end, Wuthering Waves feels unfocused, bloated, and creatively bankrupt. A broken narrative, suffocating UI clutter, shallow combat, and an overreliance on fan service make this more exhausting than entertaining. It is not deep, not tight, and not respectful of the players time. Hard pass.

text: this game had me on the fence in a few spots. i do like how the combat feels since it has weight to it so to speak. the world looks nice and traversing the map is very nice as well having a glider, wings to fly with their own stamina bar and even a bike somewhere in the progression but the bike drives like sack of bricks. the bad in the lot starts with the lack of impact within storybound bossfights. they are not properly scaled at all and it is impossible to fail. within these fights you will get characters provided for you and they are basically immortal. the hp drop no further than 1 and you can just carry on as if nothing is wrong at all. they seem to refuse to rerun old characters or even moving those into the standard-banner. many have saved up for characters that will (if all hits the fan for real) never show up again and i cant understand why. returning players will barely benefit (if at all) from the things the game provides for you. the increased drops are for the current featured characters and nothing general. even for new players it does not help at all that the new to newest characters require you to skip the story just to unlock the area so you can farm the materials to upgrade your stuff leaving you at a complete loss from there onwards and this gap will only get worse as time goes on unless it changes

text: While the game itself is fine, and the story is very good (from 2.0 onwards), many of the systems behind the game have not aged well. The free character banner is an absolute gutter trash trap, with only the worst of the 1.0 characters in it, which also happen to be the same characters as the ones you can get if you lose your 50/50 on a banner. There's been no update since, so there's absolutely 0 way to get any of the older 1.0 or 2.0 characters until Kuro decides to do a rerun on the banner, which doesn't seem to be anytime soon since they keep rereleasing Chisa's banner. Many players seemed to be banking on the 2nd Anni banner, which during 1st Anni, would let you pick a character of your choice and roll on them. Unfortunately, there seems to be no 2nd Anni banner this year, leaving even fewer routes other than begging Kuro to run more banners on older characters. So somehow after getting absolutely roasted for having a trash 1st Anni, Kuro tops it with one that's even worse. Impressive.

label: negative
text: This game feels like a harem-style hentai title and fails in nearly every aspect. The story is weak and uninspired, and the world-building is shallow, theres barely any sense of immersion or interesting lore. Male characters are almost entirely irrelevant: some male NPCs appear as villains, while playable male characters speak two lines and exist only to help you get closer to the waifu. In this world, only semi-naked women seem to matter, which further undermines immersion and makes it hard to take the lived-in world seriously. I played up to the Rinascita story for reference. Overall, this is exactly what youd expect from a fan-service game, and its surprising that a developer would put a big budget into a game that cant appeal to the average player. The graphics are decent overall, though the female character physics often look unnatural, exaggerated, or even grotesque. Beyond that, the story revolves entirely around the main character, who is bland and predictable, he seems to be the ruler of every place and deity. Combat is flashy and cinematic but ultimately shallow; its more style than substance. If you want a game with real depth, Id recommend Where Winds Meet instead, it has better combat, a much more engaging world, better monetization and a far more polished overall experience. Verdict: Stunning graphics alone cant save a shallow story, lifeless world, and uninspired gameplay. Not recommended for most players.

text: i usually dont do long reviews but im making an exception for this garbage heap of a game. started in 1.0 on mobile and only more recently on steam, so the hours wont show up on this review. the devs promised optimization that this game desperately needed and still needs in 2.4. its 3.1 and there is still absolutely none. ive spent more times than i can count waiting over five minutes for a single loading screen. if you dont have a godly device have fun with horrible graphics. 90% of the cast suffers from flat skull syndrome. technical issues aside, the story is just absolutely not it. characters get introduced and pushed aside once their banner is over. even when the focus is on them, they are almost all your typical wife/husband tropes stuffed into a pretty face. "Oh Rover, I know you have amnesia but... three hundred years ago you used to rule over the entire country and was my senpai and taught me everything i know..." just shut up dude. its embarrassing at this point. need i even talk about the poorly disguised fanservice and bad designs? and if somehow the devs manage to write a decent character (happens once every million years), their actual good lore is ignored for how much they wanna get down and dirty with the mc. gameplay is fine. i personally have a gripe with the sfx and controls but thats just me. ive had a lot of fun playing quickswap. thats about it though. TLDR if you only care for faces/bodies and pixels glazing the life out of your self insert character, youll love wuthering waves.

text: I played this game when it first released. Loved the combat, but I dropped it for sometime during the first anniversary. I wanted to get back into the game and found out it was on steam now. Finished installing and now I'm running into the dxgi_error_device_removed + dxgi_error_device_hung issue when I launch the game and start the in game download. If I have to find a work around to even be able to play the game, then the game is not even worth playing. Edit: Found a work around by lowering the refresh rate on my monitors and changing nvidia control panel settings to allow ram fallback for the gpu alongside capping framerate for background processes to 60fps

label: negative
text: 1. Optimization is Non-Existent: The technical performance is absolutely terrible. Even on a high-end device, I experience constant stuttering, FPS drops, and freezing. In a game that relies on fast-paced combat , dodge and parrying, these performance issues make the game unplayable. 2. Severe Content Drought: Aside from the technical issues, the game is incredibly boring right now. There are zero actual events. To the Developers: Releasing a new area or adding chests to the map is EXPLORATION, not an EVENT. We need actual game modes, challenges, and reasons to log in daily. Running around the map collecting chests is not enough to keep players engaged. Unless the developers fix the lag and start releasing proper events, this game will die very quickly. *Update after 400+ hours (Year 2 Anniversary):* As a Day 1 player with over 400 hours invested, it is shameful that the Year 2 anniversary is significantly worse than Year 1. The 'rewards' show a complete lack of respect for our loyalty. I am now staying strictly for the storywhich is the only redeeming quality left. My advice to new players: The writing is great, but don't spend a single cent until the developers fix the technical mess and start valuing their community.
